title: Branches, Integration, and Merge Evidence · Repository Repair Bay
summary: Integrate a short-lived change while preserving understandable topology and verifying both file content and combined behavior. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a branch-and-merge topology lab and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Create, list, rename, copy, delete, and inspect branches as movable references with upstream metadata.
Switch branches only after predicting working-tree/index carryover, untracked collisions, and sparse or worktree constraints.
Find merge bases and explain three-way integration from two tips and their best common ancestor.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
debug case — Branches main and repair diverge from merge base b00b1e5. repair changes validation while main changes output formatting; predict fast-forward possibility, resulting parents, and post-merge behavior evidence. Competency probe: Create, list, rename, copy, delete, and inspect branches as movable references with upstream metadata.

Inspect the Branch Reference trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for create, list, rename, copy, delete, and inspect branches as movable references with upstream metadata.

content.text: For Branch Reference, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Branch Reference precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Branches main and repair diverge from merge base b00b1e5. repair changes validation while main changes output formatting; predict fast-forward possibility, resulting parents, and post-merge behavior evidence. Competency probe: Create, list, rename, copy, delete, and inspect branches as movable references with upstream metadata.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A branch contains a private copy of every project file.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append a safe repository investigation headed "# Evidence: git-post-merge-verification-m9jck6" with a concrete hypothesis, one bounded simulator command, and an observation that changes the next decision.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Post Merge Verification into observable performance evidence.

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write the debug evidence note for Post Merge Verification: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Branches main and repair diverge from merge base b00b1e5. repair changes validation while main changes output formatting; predict fast-forward possibility, resulting parents, and post-merge behavior evidence. Competency probe: Verify merged tree, combined tests, log topology, diff range, and push target before publishing integration.
steps.why: Retrieving Post Merge Verification through the concrete a branch-and-merge topology lab case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Verify merged tree, combined tests, log topology, diff range, and push target before publishing integration.
